summary: Decompiled major pieces of the interface magnifier/offscreen-player indicator module. The PR replaced several ifMagnify stubs and UNK prototypes with typed implementations, updated the ifMagnify/ifMagnifyPlayer layout, added lookup/static data, and achieved a full match for ifMagnify_802FC870 plus large match improvements for ifMagnify_802FB73C, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, and ifMagnify_802FC3C0.
key_files: src/melee/if/ifmagnify.c: implemented ifMagnify_802FB73C, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, ifMagnify_802FBBDC, and ifMagnify_802FC3C0; adjusted ifMagnify_802FC870 for matching; added static lookup data and extern constants.
src/melee/if/ifmagnify.h: replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS declarations with concrete ifMagnifyPlayer, Vec2, HSD_GObj signatures and added dolphin/mtx.h for vector types.
src/melee/if/types.h: extracted ifMagnifyPlayer, changed ifMagnify to hold DynamicModelDesc* model_desc, per-player HSD_JObj/ImageDesc state, image_descs storage, and padding.
what_changed: Implemented ifMagnify_802FB73C as an edge-clamping helper: it takes an ifMagnifyPlayer and two Vec2 pointers, projects an offscreen direction vector to the screen-edge bounds using ifMagnify_804DDBxx constants, and records an edge/state value in player->state.unk.

Implemented ifMagnify_802FB8C0 as the per-player magnifier indicator render callback. It gates on HUD enabled, HUD not hidden, camera state, and player->state.is_offscreen; computes screen position with ftLib_80086A58; rotates the JObj with atan2f; translates the magnifier object; and updates colored side-arrow UI through un_802FD928/un_802FD9D8.

Implemented ifMagnify_802FBBDC as the magnifier texture/camera render pass. It resets all offscreen flags, skips ignored or non-renderable fighters, scales and retargets the CObj per fighter, computes stage-bound blend classes, blends GXColor values from Ground_801C06xx callback tables, erases the render target, draws the fighter with ftDrawCommon_80080C28 passes, copies the image with lb_800122C8, and marks the player offscreen.

Implemented ifMagnify_802FC3C0 to create or recreate each player's magnifier GObj, attach typed user data, load the joint from ifMagnify_804A1DE0.model_desc->joint, install the GX link callback, find child JObjs, share or clone HSD_ImageDesc data per slot, allocate aligned texture buffers with GXGetTexBufferSize/HSD_MemAlloc, and tint materials using player/team colors.

Adjusted ifMagnify_802FC870 to use a local HSD_Archive** from ifAll_802F3690, load the literal archive section name "lupe", create six player magnifier objects with a do-while loop, and then create the magnifier camera/render GObj.

Added data needed by the decomp: JObj assertion strings, a function-pointer table of nine Ground_801C06xx color callbacks, a packed 16-entry color-ID table, and additional ifMagnify_804DDBxx float/double externs.

Updated the headers/types so callers no longer rely on UNK signatures and so the global ifMagnify layout reflects model descriptor, per-player JObj, ImageDesc pointers, five cloned image descriptors, and explicit padding.
smart_moves: Extracting the nested player record into ifMagnifyPlayer made signatures and pointer arithmetic clearer, including the render callback deriving the slot via player - ifMagnify_804A1DE0.player.
Changing the first ifMagnify field from HSD_Joint* to DynamicModelDesc* matched how the archive section is consumed: ifMagnify_802FC3C0 loads ifMagnify_804A1DE0.model_desc->joint.

The image descriptor layout preserves per-slot behavior without over-naming unknown data: player 0 uses the archive descriptor, slots 1-5 copy it into image_descs[5 * 0x18], and each clone gets an aligned texture buffer.
Representing stage colors as a Ground function-pointer table plus a packed s32 lookup table allowed the decomp to mirror data layout while keeping the interpolation code table-driven.
The ifMagnify_802FC870 match used small codegen-sensitive choices visible in the diff: a local archive variable, the literal "lupe" section name, and a do-while loop over six slots.
The struct comments in types.h document offset evidence from existing functions, which helps future layout work avoid regressing field sizes or pointer types.
decomp_lessons: For HUD/offscreen rendering code, keep visibility gates consistent across callbacks: gm_8016AE38()->hud_enabled, ifAll_IsHUDHidden(), and Camera_80030130 all control whether the magnifier should draw or update state.
When decompiling archive-backed interface assets, distinguish the model descriptor from the loaded joint and keep texture/image descriptor ownership explicit for cloned per-player render targets.
As more functions are matched, revisit earlier type guesses: this PR changed a previous per-player HSD_TObj-style field into HSD_JObj* and separated HSD_ImageDesc* storage.
Packed data tables can be necessary for matching. The color ID table is stored as s32 values but consumed as bytes via a u8* cast.
Camera render callbacks that mutate CObj ortho/current state should save original ortho values and restore them at the end, as ifMagnify_802FBBDC does.
Do not prematurely name bitfields without evidence. The PR keeps player->state.unk despite using it for edge/arrow decisions, indicating semantics were not fully proven in the slice.
naming_conventions: Unresolved functions and data retain project address-style names such as ifMagnify_802FBBDC and ifMagnify_804DDB38.
The module uses camel-case ifMagnify naming rather than all-lowercase ifmagnify.
Concrete headers prefer address-commented prototypes with real types over UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS once signatures are known.
New inferred struct fields use descriptive snake_case names such as model_desc, image_descs, is_offscreen, and ignore_offscreen.
Unknown state remains named unk rather than being overinterpreted as direction or arrow kind.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: ifMagnify_802FC870 reached 100.00% after using a do-while loop for the six-slot initialization rather than the previous for-loop form.
Introducing HSD_Archive** archive before lbArchive_LoadSections matched codegen better than directly dereferencing ifAll_802F3690().
Using the literal section string "lupe" replaced the previous ifMagnify_804D57E8 reference in the archive load call.
Static JObj assertion strings were added for HSD_JObj operations: "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)", "jobj.h", and "jobj".
Constants were kept as extern f32/f64 ifMagnify_804DDBxx symbols instead of inlining guessed numeric names, preserving sdata/sdata2 matching behavior.
The color lookup table is declared as s32[0x10] and then byte-indexed, matching packed table layout rather than expressing it as a higher-level multidimensional byte array.
review_feedback: No human PR body, reviews, or line review comments were captured in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code at 66.41% with +120 bytes overall.
The automated report listed one new full match: main/melee/if/ifmagnify ifMagnify_802FC870 improved from 94.33% to 100.00% (+7 bytes).

The automated report listed large unmatched-item improvements: ifMagnify_802FBBDC 0.00% to 85.63% (+1726 bytes), ifMagnify_802FB8C0 0.00% to 94.04% (+748 bytes), ifMagnify_802FC3C0 0.00% to 91.33% (+548 bytes), and ifMagnify_802FB73C 0.00% to 98.87% (+383 bytes).
The automated report also showed data-section improvements for .data, .sdata2, and .sdata in main/melee/if/ifmagnify.

The merge commit message mentions intermediate work including matching ifMagnify_802FC750, removing target funcs, including references, and matching ifMagnify_802FB73C, but the final diff slice does not show a change hunk for ifMagnify_802FC750.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that name or clarify ifMagnifyPlayer.state.unk edge/arrow semantics.
Search for follow-up work that fully matches ifMagnify_802FBBDC, ifMagnify_802FB8C0, or ifMagnify_802FC3C0 after their partial improvements in PR 2450.
Look for PRs that clean up repeated Stage_GetCamBounds* calls or Player_80036978 casting in ifMagnify_802FBBDC.
Search other interface modules for DynamicModelDesc archive section patterns similar to loading "lupe".
Find HSD_ImageDesc cloning and GXGetTexBufferSize alignment patterns reused in HUD or render-to-texture decomp work.
Search for later data-section matching improvements to main/melee/if/ifmagnify .data, .sdata, and .sdata2.
